L1.area: Concurrency primitives and thread-safety marker trait implementations

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists where wrapper types providing concurrent access to data unconditionally implement the Send marker trait without requiring the inner type to also be Send. This allows non-thread-safe types to be sent across thread boundaries, potentially causing data races and undefined behavior when types with internal mutability are transferred between threads.

L3.area: Read and write ticket concurrency wrapper types and their unsafe Send marker trait implementations
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists where read ticket and write ticket wrapper types unconditionally implement the Send marker trait via unsafe impl blocks, without requiring the generic inner type parameter to also implement Send. This allows non-thread-safe types with internal mutability to be cloned and sent to other threads through these ticket types, causing data races and undefined behavior.

ground_truth.reason: ReadTicket<T> and WriteTicket<T> unconditionally implement Send without requiring T: Send, allowing non-thread-safe types to be sent across thread boundaries and causing data races.
